title: Talent Development Lead - APAC
description:
Wise is looking for a strategic and innovative Talent Development Lead to guide our talent initiatives across APAC and shape the future of Wise. In this role, you will create and implement a comprehensive talent strategy that ensures the right skills are available at the right time across our hubs in Singapore and Hyderabad.

You will foster a culture of continuous learning, working closely with senior leaders and global teams to provide high-impact development opportunities that enhance capabilities, boost performance, and support career progression.

You will oversee leadership development, new-hire onboarding, and other key programs, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, measuring the impact of training, managing budgets, and collaborating with external providers to deliver effective and engaging learning solutions. This role is based in our Singapore office.

Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement talent development strategies that align with overall company objectives and regional business needs.

  • Design, adapt, and deliver training programs to address skill gaps and reflect local market demands.

  • Collaborate with senior leadership and the people team to define key learning objectives and deliver integrated talent initiatives.

  • Oversee the design, delivery, and evaluation of training programs, including leadership development, compliance training, technical skills, and onboarding.

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing team of facilitators and learning partners, establishing them as trusted advisors throughout the employee development cycle.

  • Create and execute a strategic plan aligned with our three core pillars: building skills for the future, evolving our ways of working, and delivering an irresistible employee experience.

  • Champion a culture of inclusive, continuous learning and professional development across the region.

  • Work closely with our talent management and talent acquisition teams to deliver a holistic approach to talent at wise.

  • Manage external training provider relationships, including contract negotiation and assessing the effectiveness of external solutions.

Qualifications

Background and expertise

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent, ideally in adult learning, business management, or a related field.

  • Proven experience in learning and development, talent development, or training within a service-oriented organisation, ideally within the fintech, financial services, or tech industry

  • Demonstrated success as a people leader, including the ability to inspire and guide regionally dispersed teams.

Core skills

  • Strong proficiency in project management and design thinking.

  • Consultative approach to problem-solving, applying data-driven insights to create tailored solutions.

  • Confidence in interpreting data and producing data-led, visually compelling presentations.

  • Organised, detail-focused, and able to work under pressure while maintaining accuracy.

  • Adept at stakeholder management, communicating effectively with diverse teams to encourage collaboration.

  • Experience in virtual and international environments, including hybrid teams.

  • Excellent command of English; additional language skills are an advantage.

Key attributes

  • Aware of important developments within the business and industry trends relating to people and organisational development.

  • Strategic mindset in developing talent initiatives, understanding the bigger picture and how individual actions fit into the broader suite of talent development services.

  • Comfortable navigating uncertainty and rapid change; adaptable to evolving business needs.

  • Proactive and self-starting, anticipating potential challenges or opportunities and proposing solutions.

  • Willingness to contribute beyond one’s own role, collaborating with colleagues to achieve collective objectives.

  • Enthusiasm for collecting, processing, and applying data to streamline workflows and drive successful team projects.

  • Excellent communication skills and ability to coordinate large-scale initiatives.

  • Meticulous attention to detail and a passion for high-quality, visually appealing deliverables.

postedCompany.description:
Who we are

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move money around the world. With the Wise account people and businesses can hold over 40 currencies, move money between countries and spend money abroad. Large companies and banks use Wise technology too; an entirely new cross-border payments network that will one day power money without borders for everyone, everywhere. However you use the platform, Wise is on a mission to make your life easier and save you money.

Co-founded by Kristo Käärmann and Taavet Hinrikus, Wise launched in 2011 under its original name TransferWise. It is one of the world’s fastest growing, profitable technology companies and is listed on the London Stock Exchange under the ticker, WISE.

16 million people and businesses use Wise. In fiscal year 2023, Wise processed approximately £105 billion in cross-border transactions, saving customers around £1.5 billion.

What it’s really like to work here

At Wise, we do things a bit differently. There’s no corporate nonsense, and no old-fashioned hierarchy. Instead, we work in dozens of self-sufficient, autonomous teams. Think of them like start-ups within a start-up that learn from each other.

Each team picks the problems they want to solve. So there’s no micro-management. No hiding behind fancy job titles. And no one telling you what to do. You are your own boss. But you’ll get tons of guidance and plenty of support from talented, super-smart colleagues from all over Wise.

We’re going to be upfront — the way we work doesn’t suit everyone. But if freedom, autonomy, and life-affirming, head-scratching professional challenges rock your world, we could be a match made in heaven.
postedCompany.name: WISE ASIA-PACIFIC PTE. LTD.
